pet.name: bb
pet.special_needs: Needs complex enrichment, supervised activity, stable feeding times, and close observation of stress-related behavior.
pet.characteristics: Golden Retrievers are highly social, intelligent dogs requiring consistent mental and physical enrichment through play, training, and interactive activities. They thrive on stable daily routines including regular feeding times, moderate to vigorous exercise, and close human interaction. These dogs are sensitive to stress and environmental changes; they benefit from predictable schedules, calm handling, and monitoring for anxiety-related behaviors. They require regular grooming due to their double coat, routine veterinary care, and a balanced diet appropriate to their activity level and age. Early socialization and ongoing training support behavioral stability.
pet.species_profile_summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years in healthy captive conditions and require consistent enrichment, routine, and attentive behavioral monitoring.

age_context.summary: Golden Retrievers typically live 10-12 years in healthy captive conditions and require consistent enrichment, routine, and attentive behavioral monitoring.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Puppy and Kitten Routine Basics
retrieved_passages.content: Exercise and enrichment for very young pets should stay short and positive. Puppies often do better with several brief walks or training moments instead of one long outing, and kittens benefit from short interactive play sessions that stop before exhaustion.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning feeding and water check
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide measured meal at consistent time; observe appetite and water intake as part of daily monitoring.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Stable feeding times support routine and allow early detection of appetite changes in stress-sensitive dogs.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening feeding and water check
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide measured meal at consistent time; observe appetite and water intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Second stable feeding time maintains predictable routine and enables consistent behavioral observation.

raw_recommendations.name: Brief enrichment play or sniff walk
raw_recommendations.notes: Short interactive play, sniff walk, or training moment; keep positive and stop before exhaustion.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Short, consistent enrichment improves quality of life and supports mental stimulation without large time blocks.

raw_recommendations.name: Afternoon enrichment play or training
raw_recommendations.notes: Interactive play, training session, or calm interaction; keep brief and positive.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Multiple brief enrichment sessions suit active, intelligent Goldens better than one long outing and support stable routine.

raw_recommendations.name: Daily observation log: energy, bathroom, stress signs
raw_recommendations.notes: Record energy level, bathroom habits, and any stress-related behaviors; note any changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Short, specific observation tasks create a record for stress-sensitive dogs and enable early detection of behavioral or health changes.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly brushing and coat check
raw_recommendations.notes: Brush double coat; check for mats, skin issues, or parasites.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Golden Retrievers require regular grooming due to their double coat; brushing also supports calm handling and bonding.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly wellness check and routine review
raw_recommendations.notes: Review observation logs for patterns; check ears, nails, and overall condition; note any concerns for veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Routine review of monitoring records supports early detection of changes and informs veterinary care decisions.
